calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-04-2006 Off Calendar

Ntc Of Mtn To Require Plntf To Post Security And Declaring Plntf To Be Vexatious Litigant And For Prefiling Injunction Prohibiting Plntf From Filing Any New Litigation
Off Calendar May-30-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Tera M. Heintz, Esq., of Morgan Lewis & Bockius LLP, 442-1334, for Defendant and Moving Party San Francisco Food Banks
RULING:
NO ORAL ARGUMENT PRESENT. NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO FOOD BANK'S Motion To Require Plaintiff To Post Security And Declaring Plaintiff To Be Vexatious Litigant And For Prefiling Injunction Prohibiting Plaintiff From Filing Any New Litigation; In light of Wolfe I and given the allegations in this case and those of Wolfe I, the Court accepted the motions as submitted on the papers. The court took the matter under submission. (301/JLW/dm) Judge James L. Warren, Reporter Christina Paxton, CSR #1558.

Notice Of America'S Second Harvest'S Motion For An Order: 1) Declaring Pltf To Be A Vexatious Litigant; 2) Requiring Pltf To Post Security; And 3) For A Pre-Filing Injunction Prohibiting New Litigation
Off Calendar May-30-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
RULING:
NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT AMERICA'S SECOND HARVEST - THE NATION'S FOOD BANK'S Motion For An Order: 1) Declaring Plaintiff To Be A Vexatious Litigant; 2) Requiring Plaintiff To Post Security; And 3) For A Pre-Filing Injunction Prohibiting New Litigation; In light of Wolfe I and given the allegations in this case and those of Wolfe I, the Court accepted the motions as submitted on the papers. The court took the matter under submission. (301/JLW/dm) Judge James L. Warren, Reporter Christina Paxton, CSR #1558.

Notice Of America'S Second Harvest'S Motion For An Order: 1) Declaring Pltf To Be A Vexatious Litigant; 2) Requiring Pltf To Post Security; And 3) For A Pre-Filing Injunction Prohibiting New Litigation
Off Calendar May-01-2006 Continued To May-16-06 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Burton H. Wolfe, 921-5629, Plaintiff in Propria Persona
Tera M. Heintz, Esq., and Laura J. Remington, Esq., of Morgan Lewis 442-1095, for Defendant San Francisco Food Bank
John R. Hurley, Esq., of Piper Rudnick Gray Cary, 836-2528, for Defendant and America's Second Harvest
RULING:
DEFENDANT AMERICA'S SECOND HARVEST - THE NATION'S FOOD BANK'S Motion For An Order: 1) Declaring Plaintiff To Be A Vexatious Litigant; 2) Requiring Plaintiff To Post Security; And 3) For A Pre-Filing Injunction Prohibiting New Litigation was continued to 5/16/06 on the court's own motion. Opposition is due by 5/9/06 and Reply by 5/12/06 with courtesy copies to Dept. 301. (301/JLW/dm) Judge James L. Warren, Reporter Christina Paxton, CSR #1558.

RULING:
DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO FOOD BANK'S Motion To Require Plaintiff To Post Security And Declaring Plaintiff To Be Vexatious Litigant And For Prefiling Injunction Prohibiting Plaintiff From Filing Any New Litigation was continued to 5/16/06 on the court's own motion.
